title: Taste fact
description: Structured storage contract for taste/facts.jsonl. One fact = one (scope, signal) pair with supporting evidence. Replaces the binary 3-rejection-flag logic in system.md with a multi-signal taste profile that distinguishes global preferences from project-specific ones and survives decay + reactivation.

id.description: ULID (Crockford-base32, 26 chars). Time-sortable: the prefix encodes creation millis, so a sorted facts.jsonl is roughly chronological.

scope.description: Where the fact applies. Reads are filtered by scope before score adjustment — a project-scoped fact never leaks to other projects.

level.description: global = applies everywhere. project = only inside the repo where it was captured. component_type = e.g. 'dashboard', 'pricing', 'landing'. archetype = one of the 12 brand archetypes (Ruler, Sage, etc.).

key.description: Scope selector. '*' for global. Project key is the project-root directory basename. component_type uses the names from context-inference.md Step 2.5. archetype uses lowercase archetype names.

signal.description: What the user prefers or avoids. Read by Step 4.5 of the style-selection algorithm.

direction.description: avoid → negative score adjustment + optional hard-block when permanent. prefer → positive score adjustment.

target_type.description: What dimension of the candidate the signal matches against. style_id is the strongest (exact match). pattern is free-text and only used by the soft score adjustment — never for hard-blocks.

target_value.description: The value to match. For style_id: the canonical id from _index.json. For motion_tier: one of Static|Subtle|Expressive|Kinetic. For pattern: a short phrase such as 'glassmorphism-on-dashboards'.

evidence.description: Chronologically-ordered trail of signals that support this fact. Promotion and decay rules (taste-aging.mjs) count kinds and most-recent dates from here.

kind.description: Source of the signal. explicit_* = user said so in chat. git_* = harvest-git-signal inferred it from repo activity. pairwise_pick = user picked one variant over others via /variants.

quote_or_diff.description: Short verbatim quote from the user (for explicit_*), truncated diff summary (for git_*), or variant ids (for pairwise_pick). Preserved so future aggregators can re-interpret.

at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the signal.

confidence.description: How much weight Step 4.5 applies to this fact. Heuristic signals start at 0.5-0.7, explicit strong language at 0.8-0.9. Decay halves this on each aging run with no new evidence. Facts below 0.2 are deleted.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the fact was first created. Immutable.

last_seen.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent evidence entry. Aging rules compare this against now() to decide decay.

flag.description: Lifecycle state. active = default, counts in scoring with confidence weight. permanent = hard-block when direction=avoid (takes precedence over confidence). decayed = no new evidence for 30+ days, confidence halved, hidden from scoring until reactivated.
